nodes.label: AI as tool for lesson planning
nodes.label_negative: AI as ready-to-use lesson plan provider
nodes.grounding_span: I sometimes use AI to write lesson plans, but usually have to do a lot of tweaking

nodes.label: AI as template and synonym tool
nodes.grounding_span: I've also used AI to create templates for letters of recommendation, or to ask for synonyms

nodes.label: AI understanding of musical terminology
nodes.label_negative: AI irrelevance to hands-on music teaching
nodes.grounding_span: I am a music teacher, so a lot of what I do is tangible, hands-on activities

nodes.label: AI use for personalized student instruction
nodes.label_negative: AI as unnecessary or ineffective for student support
nodes.grounding_span: knowing that students that need the extra examples or support will have it

nodes.label: AI as task delegator for complete tasks
nodes.label_negative: AI as time-consuming or unnecessary task
nodes.grounding_span: letting it take over tasks that would leave me more time to work on other things

nodes.label: AI as project organization tool
nodes.grounding_span: I also think I can use it as an organization tool when it comes to documents

nodes.label: Efficiency and time savings

nodes.label: Indifference to workplace AI pressure
